heroHeadline: PLA Sorties Rebound to 8 on Apr 25 After Post-Anniversary Lull — 7 PLAN Vessels Stable; Taiwan Locked In $6.58B US Arms Deals Apr 23; 19 Days to Trump-Xi Summit
heroSubtitle: Taiwan's MND detected 8 PLA aircraft sorties and 7 PLAN vessels on April 25, 2026 — a moderate rebound from the 2-sortie post-anniversary lull of April 24, with the 7 PLAN vessel count remaining stable at elevated post-Liaoning levels. The April 25 uptick continues PLA's oscillating pre-summit pressure cycle: Liaoning carrier transit April 20 (12 PLAN vessels) → 24-sortie non-exercise peak April 21 → 6 sorties April 22 → 15-sortie PLA Navy 77th anniversary spike April 23 → 2-sortie post-anniversary lull April 24 → 8-sortie rebound April 25. Separately, Taiwan formalized $6.58 billion in US arms procurement agreements on April 23 — including HIMARS and ammunition co-production — locking in production queue placement despite the ongoing NT$1.25T special defense budget deadlock (KMT insisting on NT$800B cap). The $14B PAC-3/NASAMS package remains on hold pending the May 14-15 Trump-Xi Beijing summit (19 days away), which Xi has flagged as an occasion to press Trump on limiting arms sales. With the summit approaching, PLA appears to be sustaining a moderate-pressure posture — elevated enough to signal resolve, but calibrated to avoid a sustained high-sortie campaign that could create pre-summit diplomatic liability.
footerNote: Data sourced from Taiwan MND, US DoD, DSCA, CSIS ChinaPower, Global Taiwan Institute, AIT, Reuters, AP, Bloomberg, CNBC, NPR, Taipei Times, Focus Taiwan, Nikkei Asia, Japan Times, ANI News, Tribune India, Vision Times, South China Morning Post, Taiwan News, Asia Times, The Asia Cable, GlobalSecurity.org, Xinhua, CGTN, The Diplomat, Washington Times, Global Times, People's Daily Online, Al Jazeera, The Manila Times, CNBC, MarineLink, Stars and Stripes, US News & World Report, Free Malaysia Today, Military Times, Foundation for Defense of Democracies, and institutional analyses. All claims reflect multi-pole sourcing across Western, East Asian, and international media.
